sonnet_review.biasedText: On Thursday, the Brazilian venue hosting the conference burst into flames from what was likely an electrical fire.
sonnet_review.reason: While I flagged 'ham-fisted metaphor' separately, Sonnet's point that the author uses the fire incident as a rhetorical device to sensationalize COP's failures is defensible. The framing is part of the author's editorial approach.

sonnet_review.biasedText: delegates from drowning, subsistence-farming volcanic archipelagos in the South Pacific humbly pleaded
sonnet_review.reason: This matches my detection of the same passage. The emotionally loaded descriptors 'drowning,' 'humbly pleaded' are editorializing in the author's voice.

sonnet_review.biasedText: a new kind of cynical resignation to this future
sonnet_review.reason: I did not flag this exact phrase, but 'cynical resignation' is indeed a loaded characterization of alternative climate positions, implying defeatism and bad faith.

sonnet_review.biasedText: This forfeit, recently championed by Bill Gates, flies under the banner of 'climate realism' or, more sunnily, 'climate pragmatism.'
sonnet_review.reason: I did not flag 'forfeit' specifically, but it is indeed a pejorative word choice that frames climate pragmatism as giving up, which is editorializing.

sonnet_review.biasedText: the purveyors of this kind of 'pragmatism' are downright oblivious to the implications of a 3-degrees-warmer world
sonnet_review.reason: This matches my detection. The author dismisses pragmatism advocates as 'downright oblivious' rather than engaging substantively with their arguments.

sonnet_review.biasedText: If warming the planet beyond 1 degree Celsius isn't safe, then 3 degrees is madness.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of the same text, where I classified it as opinion presented as fact. The word 'madness' is emotionally charged editorial language.

sonnet_review.biasedText: COP itself has become an annual punching bag and synecdoche for climate inaction more broadly.
sonnet_review.reason: While I didn't flag this exact phrase, the characterization of COP as a 'punching bag' and the framing of broader 'climate inaction' is editorially negative and contributes to an overall negative framing.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Everything you've read above, the relentlessly dour litany of climate threats and the meditation on the intransigence of climate politics, has also been spun—by commentators availed of the same set of facts—as a success story.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection. The use of 'spun' to describe optimistic interpretations frames them as distortions, while presenting the author's alarming framing as the default truth.

gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection exactly. The headline's 'Almost-Apocalyptic' is alarmist framing.
Matches my detection. 'Ham-fisted' is a subjective, editorial adjective.

gpt_review.biasedText: COP has frequently been a ritual in frustration and futility, ending with a set of pledges and promises that have rarely gone as far as scientists say they need to, followed by weeks of postmortem finger-pointing and self-flagellation.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage. The characterization is editorial and presented as established fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: heavily compromised text that does little to materially steer the planet off fossil fuels.
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this exact phrase, but 'heavily compromised' and 'does little to materially steer' are judgmental characterizations that go beyond neutral reporting.

gpt_review.biasedText: drowning, subsistence-farming volcanic archipelagos in the South Pacific humbly pleaded with countries such as Saudi Arabia and Russia to pledge to someday stop pumping their oceans of oil
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. Heavily editorialized with emotionally loaded framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: This forfeit, recently championed by Bill Gates, flies under the banner of 'climate realism' or, more sunnily, 'climate pragmatism.' In this view, the trade-offs between minimizing global warming and pursuing other goals for humanity are too steep, and the consequences of somewhat-checked warming will be manageable. If climate negotiators were naive about the political economy of the energy transition when COP started 30 years ago, though, then the purveyors of this kind of 'pragmatism' are downright oblivious to the implications of a 3-degrees-warmer world that they've made conceptual peace with.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'downright oblivious' and includes additional biased framing like 'forfeit.'

gpt_review.biasedText: If warming the planet beyond 1 degree Celsius isn't safe, then 3 degrees is madness.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. 'Madness' is loaded editorial language.
Matches my detection of 'the sea succumbs to merciless heat waves.' 'Merciless' is emotionally loaded anthropomorphizing language.
I didn't flag this specific phrase, but 'submit to' is anthropomorphizing and dramatic language beyond neutral description.

gpt_review.biasedText: this vanishing act might extend to the Amazon rainforest, which—already relentlessly pared back by deforestation—could submit to a runaway drying.
gpt_review.reason: 'Relentlessly pared back' and 'vanishing act' are editorially loaded, though deforestation of the Amazon is factual. The subjective framing makes this a defensible detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: insurance markets may all but collapse—even in supposed climate refuges such as Minnesota.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this, but 'supposed climate refuges' has a mocking, dismissive tone that editorializes.

gpt_review.biasedText: the UN projects that current policies will result in almost 3 degrees Celsius of warming by 2100. Unfortunately, that 1.5-degree benchmark wasn't selected at random.
gpt_review.reason: The interjection of 'Unfortunately' is an explicit authorial value judgment inserted into what should be factual reporting.

gpt_review.biasedText: Still, this is an actuarial risk you wouldn't take with a new house, much less with the only known habitable planet in the universe.
gpt_review.reason: This is a rhetorical analogy designed to dramatize the stakes. It's editorial persuasion rather than neutral analysis.

gpt_review.biasedText: The Earth, of course, is indifferent to what's politically possible, and where it's headed is still dangerous for humanity.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. The author asserts danger as unqualified fact while using rhetorical flourish.

gpt_review.biasedText: incredibly, maddeningly difficult. It will be ugly. There will be losers. Ultimately, though, there will be many more winners.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact passage, but the emotive language is editorial rather than analytical.

gpt_review.biasedText: the biggest chemistry experiment on the planet in 66 million years, and one of the fastest derangements of the carbon cycle in the age of animal life.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. 'Derangements' is a charged, non-neutral word choice.

sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The headline uses 'Almost-Apocalyptic' as alarmist framing. Sonnet missed this prominent instance.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: even the starchy World Bank has warned

sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The sarcastic juxtaposition used to undercut climate progress was missed by Sonnet.
